mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits easier financial conditions to equities through a lower discount rate and reduced funding stress, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is validated when rate cuts persist rather than reverse within the same month.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive on average after month-over-month FEDFUNDS declines, or if the hit rate stays below 50% across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden rise in UNRATE above a short-run threshold signals labor-market deterioration, reduced household income growth, and weaker earnings expectations, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is strongest when the unemployment increase is broad and not offset by simultaneous payroll gains.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the signal loses explanatory power after controlling for PAYEMS changes.

mechanism_narrative: A broad acceleration in RSAFS indicates stronger nominal consumption demand, supporting revenue expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal fails if sales growth is concentrated only in inflation without real demand improvement.
failure_condition: Reject if higher RSAFS growth does not predict higher 60-day SPX returns after adjusting for CPIAUCSL, or if the return spread between top and bottom RSAFS-growth quintiles is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: An upswing in INDPRO after a prior slowdown signals improving industrial activity and inventory restocking, which should support S&P 500 returns over the next month. The mechanism is invalid when production gains come from price effects without volume confirmation.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises for 2 consecutive months after a prior 3-month decline
failure_condition: Reject if the specified production-rebound pattern is followed by negative 20-day SPX returns in a majority of instances, or if the average forward return is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is already elevated, additional increases tighten discount rates and liquidity conditions, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week. The signal activates only after policy rates cross a restrictive threshold.
failure_condition: Reject if SPX delivers positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of periods with FEDFUNDS above 4.5%, or if the average forward return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A strong housing rebound reflected in HOUST combined with rising retail sales indicates demand stabilization and better cyclical growth, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal requires both variables to improve together because housing alone can rebound without broad demand strength.
failure_condition: Reject if joint increases in HOUST and RSAFS do not produce positive 60-day SPX returns on average, or if either series alone explains the effect without the interaction.

mechanism_narrative: A rising PAYEMS trend signals payroll expansion and income growth, supporting consumption and earnings expectations, which should raise S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect should weaken when unemployment is simultaneously rising, because payroll gains then reflect lagged data rather than actual strength.
failure_condition: Reject if payroll-growth episodes fail to generate positive 20-day SPX returns, or if the effect persists even when UNRATE is also rising materially.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains low, real policy rates fall and financial conditions ease, supporting equity multiples and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal fails if inflation rises because of demand destruction rather than benign monetary accommodation.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises above its 6-month average while FEDFUNDS stays below 3%
failure_condition: Reject if low-rate inflation episodes are not followed by positive 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign turns negative when FEDFUNDS remains below 3%.
